title: 10 best herbal tinctures for frizzy hair
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for frizzy hair are concentrated liquid extracts made from herbs that are known to promote hair health and reduce frizz. They are a popular natural alternative to commercial hair care products that contain harsh chemicals. Herbal tinctures like rosemary, lavender, and horsetail can help to nourish the scalp, promote hair growth, and reduce frizz by strengthening the hair shaft and sealing in moisture. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can improve the health and appearance of their hair, leading to increased confidence and self-esteem. For example, a person who struggled with frizzy, unmanageable hair may find that using a rosemary tincture helps to smooth their hair and give it a healthy shine, allowing them to feel more confident and professional in their daily life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with frizzy hair because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can improve the health of your hair and scalp. The tincture is made from the yarrow plant, which is rich in vitamins and minerals that can nourish your hair, reduce scalp inflammation, and promote hair growth. By using yarrow tincture regularly, you can help to strengthen your hair, reduce frizz, and add shine and softness to your locks. Its natural ingredients can also help to soothe a dry, itchy scalp and improve the overall condition of your hair, leaving it looking and feeling healthier and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones like costunolide and dehydrocostus lactone, can help soothe an irritated scalp, reducing frizz caused by inflammation.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tincture, from tannins and flavonoids, can help to tighten the scalp and reduce oil production, preventing frizz caused by greasy hair.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of essential oils like azulene and camphor, can help to maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can lead to frizzy hair.

Promotes hair growth: Yarrow tincture can stimulate hair growth, thanks to its content of plant sterols and flavonoids, which can help to improve circulation and nourish the hair follicles, reducing frizz caused by weak or damaged hair.
Moisturizing: The moisturizing properties of yarrow tincture, from its mucilaginous polysaccharides, can help to hydrate the hair and scalp, reducing frizz caused by dryness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in volatile oils, yarrow flowers help to nourish and smooth the hair follicles, reducing frizz.
Leaves: High in vitamins and minerals, yarrow leaves promote hair strength and elasticity, helping to tame frizz.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, yarrow seeds help to moisturize and condition the hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine.
Stems: Packed with antioxidants, yarrow stems protect the hair from environmental stressors, preventing frizz and damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, rinse, and let dry for 24 hours.
Chop the dried yarrow and place it in a clean jar, then add 8 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof).
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the finished yarrow tincture in a glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to a year. Apply a few drops to frizzy hair daily or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with frizzy hair because it contains a powerful compound called artemisinin, which can help to naturally smooth and tame unruly hair. This is due to its ability to nourish and strengthen the hair follicles, while also promoting healthy hair growth. Additionally, wormwood tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm a dry, itchy scalp, which can often contribute to frizziness. By using wormwood tincture as a natural hair treatment, you can help to restore your hair's natural shine and luster, while also reducing frizz and promoting overall hair health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, can help reduce scalp inflammation, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing frizz.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of wormwood tincture, from active constituents such as thujone and isothujone, can help maintain a clean and healthy scalp, reducing factors that may contribute to frizzy hair.

Promotes blood circulation: Wormwood tincture stimulates blood circulation in the scalp, thanks to compounds like chamazulene, ensuring proper nutrient delivery to hair follicles and reducing frizz caused by poor scalp health.

Hair growth stimulation: The hair growth-promoting properties of wormwood tincture, attributed to active constituents like beta-sitosterol, can help strengthen hair follicles and reduce frizz by promoting healthier, stronger hair growth.
Scalp tonic: Wormwood tincture acts as a scalp tonic, regulating sebum production and maintaining the right balance of moisture in the scalp, reducing frizz caused by excessive dryness or oiliness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients, the leaves help to nourish and smooth frizzy hair.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants that can help to promote hair growth and reduce frizz.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, seeds can moisturize and strengthen hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of fresh wormwood herb, then submerge it in a clean glass jar filled with 10 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol.
Seal the jar and let it steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, occasionally shaking the mixture.
After steeping, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Pour the liquid into a dark glass bottle, tightly sealing it to store.
To use, dilute 1-2 ml of the tincture in water and apply it evenly on frizzy hair, massaging it gently into the scalp before rinsing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with frizzy hair because it contains high levels of vitamin E and antioxidants that nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can also help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can contribute to frizz. Regular use of pot marigold tincture can improve the hair's overall health, making it more manageable and less prone to frizz. Additionally, the tincture's natural emollients help to smooth the hair cuticle, sealing in moisture and preventing flyaways. These benefits make pot marigold tincture a valuable addition to any hair care routine for those looking to tame frizz and promote healthy, beautiful hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture can help reduce inflammation of the scalp, which can help prevent frizzy hair caused by irritation and dryness. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Moisturizing: Pot marigold tincture has moisturizing properties that can help hydrate the hair and reduce frizz. This is due to the high content of mucilage in the plant.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tincture contains antioxidants that can help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizzy hair. The antioxidant properties come from the flavonoids and carotenoids present in the plant.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help keep the scalp healthy and free from infection, which can help prevent frizzy hair caused by scalp issues. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as limonene and camphor.

Circulation-boosting: Pot marigold tincture can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, which can promote healthy hair growth and reduce frizz. This is due to the presence of thiamin and nicotinic acid.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers of the pot marigold are used for their anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help tame frizzy hair.
Leaves: Packed with antioxidants, the leaves are used in tea to nourish the hair, promote hair growth, and reduce frizz.
Seeds: The seeds contain essential oils that help to condition the hair, making it more manageable and less prone to frizz.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 oz of dried pot marigold flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 8 oz of vodka or rum over the flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean glass bottle and use a few drops on frizzy hair after washing and conditioning.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower tincture helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and nourish the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The tincture can also help to balance the pH level of the hair, which can reduce frizz and add shine. Additionally, cornflower is known to strengthen hair follicles, preventing breakage and promoting thicker, fuller hair. Its emollient properties can help to smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and leaving hair looking smooth and sleek. Regular use of cornflower tincture can help to tame unruly, frizzy hair, leaving it looking healthy and manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cornflower tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe an irritated scalp, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing frizz, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help to tighten the scalp and reduce excess oil production, which can lead to frizziness. This is due to the tannins present in cornflower.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in cornflower tincture protects the hair from environmental damage and oxidative stress, which can cause frizz. The antioxidants include various flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Moisturizing: Cornflower tincture helps to lock in moisture, preventing dryness and reducing frizz. This is facilitated by mucilaginous compounds found in cornflower.

Circulation-boosting: By improving blood flow to the scalp, cornflower tincture promotes healthy hair growth and reduces frizz. This is achieved through the presence of compounds like flavonoids and essential oils in cornflower.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, cornflower flowers help to nourish the scalp and reduce frizz by promoting hair strength and elasticity.
Leaves: High in vitamins and minerals, cornflower leaves help to moisturize and condition the hair, reducing frizz and promoting shine.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and proteins, cornflower seeds help to strengthen and repair damaged hair, reducing frizz and promoting growth.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 oz of dried cornflower petals and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 8 oz of vodka or rum over the cornflower petals, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the specified time, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent petals.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. To use, mix a few drops with water and apply to frizzy hair before styling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in silica and other minerals that can strengthen hair follicles and promote hair growth. Silica, in particular, helps to strengthen hair strands, making them less prone to breakage and damage that can lead to frizz. Additionally, horsetail tincture can help to improve the overall health and condition of the hair, leaving it looking smoother, shinier, and more manageable. By nourishing the hair from the inside out, horsetail tincture can help to reduce frizz and promote healthy, beautiful hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: This property helps reduce scalp inflammation, which can be a cause of frizzy hair. The active constituents responsible for this property are the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in horsetail.
Promotes collagen production: Horsetail tincture promotes collagen production, which can strengthen hair and reduce frizz. The high silica content in horsetail is responsible for this property.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant property of horsetail tincture helps protect hair from free radical damage, which can cause frizz. The antioxidants in horsetail include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.

Helps balance scalp oil production: Horsetail tincture helps balance scalp oil production, which can reduce frizzy hair. The astringent properties of horsetail, due to its tannin content, contribute to this balancing effect.

Promotes hair growth: Horsetail tincture promotes hair growth, which can help reduce frizzy hair by ensuring healthy, strong hair growth. The high silica and selenium content in horsetail contribute to this property.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica and other minerals, which can help to strengthen hair and reduce frizz by promoting healthy hair growth.
Stems: The stems of horsetail plants contain high levels of silica, which can help to smooth the hair cuticle and reduce frizz, as well as promote hair growth and strength.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves contain flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds that can help to promote healthy hair growth and reduce frizz by strengthening the hair shaft.

Seeds: While not commonly used in medicinal teas, horsetail seeds contain essential fatty acids and other nutrients that can help to nourish the hair and scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried horsetail herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the herbs until they are completely covered.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer into a clean glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name of the tincture and the date it was made, and store in a cool, dark place. Use a few drops of the tincture on your frizzy hair daily or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with frizzy hair because it is rich in nutrients and antioxidants that promote healthy hair growth. It contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can often be the culprit behind frizzy hair. Fennel is also known to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, which can help strengthen hair follicles and reduce hair breakage. Additionally, fennel tincture can help balance the scalp's oil production, preventing it from becoming too greasy or too dry. This can lead to smoother, more manageable hair that is less prone to frizz. Overall, fennel tincture can be a natural and effective solution for those looking to tame frizzy hair and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like anethole, fenchone, and limonene.

Antioxidant: Fennel tincture contains antioxidants that can help protect the hair from environmental stressors, reducing frizz. The antioxidant properties come from flavonoids, phenolic acids, and vitamins like vitamin C.
Moisturizing: Fennel tincture can help moisturize the hair and scalp, reducing frizz caused by dryness. This is due to the presence of sugars, amino acids, and fatty acids in fennel.
Promotes Hair Growth: Fennel tincture can help promote hair growth, which can reduce the appearance of frizz. This is due to the presence of phytoestrogens, which can help stimulate hair growth.

Strengthens Hair: Fennel tincture can help strengthen the hair, reducing breakage and split ends that can contribute to frizz. This is due to the presence of minerals like calcium, potassium, and iron, as well as the amino acids in fennel.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in antioxidants and essential oils that help to nourish the scalp and reduce frizz by promoting hair growth and improving hair elasticity.

Leaves: Fennel leaves contain high levels of vitamins and minerals that help to strengthen hair follicles, reduce hair breakage, and promote healthy hair growth, resulting in smoother and more manageable hair.
Flowers: Fennel flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe an irritated scalp, reduce dandruff, and promote healthy hair growth, resulting in less frizz and more shine.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes are rich in nutrients and antioxidants that help to improve blood circulation in the scalp, promote hair growth, and reduce frizz, leaving hair looking smooth and shiny.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried fennel seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the fennel seeds until fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solids.
Store the resulting fennel tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use a few drops on frizzy hair before styling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tincture helps with frizzy hair because it contains saponins, which are natural cleansers that can gently remove dirt and excess oils from the scalp and hair. Additionally, it has conditioning properties that can help to detangle and smooth frizzy hair, leaving it looking shiny and feeling soft. The tincture can also improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing hair fall. Furthermore, it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to soothe an itchy scalp and prevent dandruff, which can contribute to frizzy hair. Overall, using English ivy tincture can help to tame frizz and promote healthy, manageable hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: English ivy tincture can help reduce scalp inflammation, which can be a cause of frizzy hair. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins, flavonoids, and polyacetylenes.
Hair conditioning property: English ivy tincture can help moisturize and condition the hair, reducing frizz. This is due to the presence of mucilage, a substance that can help seal in moisture.

Hair growth promotion property: English ivy tincture can stimulate hair growth, which can help reduce the appearance of frizz. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and flavonoids, which can improve blood circulation in the scalp.

Dandruff reduction property: English ivy tincture can help reduce dandruff, which can contribute to frizzy hair. This is due to the antifungal and antibacterial properties of the tincture, which can help control the overgrowth of Malassezia, a fungus that can cause dandruff.

Hair detangling property: English ivy tincture can help detangle hair, reducing breakage and frizz. This is due to the mucilage and saponins in the tincture, which can help smooth the hair cuticle and reduce friction between hair strands.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and conditioning agents, leaves help to smooth frizzy hair and add shine.
Stems: Contain flexible fibers that can help to strengthen hair and reduce breakage, promoting healthy hair growth.
Rhizomes: Packed with antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhizomes can help to soothe scalp irritations and promote a healthy scalp environment for hair growth.
Flowers: Infused with vitamins and minerals, flowers can help to nourish and protect hair from environmental stressors, reducing frizz and promoting soft, manageable hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest English ivy leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides; rinse and dry them.
Chop the leaves finely, then fill a clean jar halfway with the chopped leaves and add 80-proof vodka until the jar is full.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After four to six weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Label the bottle with the contents and date, then store it in a cool, dark place; apply a few drops of the tincture to frizzy hair before styling for smoothness and shine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with frizzy hair because it contains beneficial compounds that can improve the condition of hair. The tincture is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect hair from damage caused by environmental factors, such as pollution and UV rays. Additionally, St. John's wort has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce scalp inflammation and improve the health of the hair follicles. This can lead to stronger, healthier hair that is less prone to frizz and more resistant to damage. Furthermore, St. John's wort has been shown to promote blood circulation in the scalp, which can help to nourish the hair and promote growth. All of these factors make St. John's wort tincture a valuable addition to any hair care routine, particularly for those struggling with frizzy hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture can reduce inflammation, which can help prevent frizzy hair caused by scalp inflammation, due to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of St. John's wort tincture can protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, keeping the hair healthy and reducing frizz, thanks to the active constituents like hypericin, rutin, and quercetin.
Nourishing: St. John's wort tincture can nourish the hair, promoting hair growth and reducing frizz, due to its essential fatty acids, flavonoids, and tannins.
Moisturizing: The moisturizing properties of St. John's wort tincture can help hydrate the hair, preventing dryness and reducing frizz, as it contains tannins, mucilage, and waxes.

Soothing: St. John's wort tincture can soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and dandruff, which can contribute to frizzy hair, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, mainly from hypericin and hyperforin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help improve hair health and reduce frizz.
Leaves: Rich in nutrients and flavonoids, which can strengthen hair follicles and promote hair growth, reducing frizziness.
Stems: Contain beneficial compounds that can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids and vitamins, which can nourish the hair and scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, rinse, and dry thoroughly.
Fill a clean jar with dried herb to 3/4 full, then add 80 proof vodka until herb is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid.
Store tincture in a glass dropper bottle and use 10-15 drops on frizzy hair before styling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with frizzy hair because it contains properties that can tame flyaways and add shine to your locks. It is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and condition the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The tincture can also help to seal in moisture, reducing frizz and leaving your hair looking smooth and silky. Additionally, lavender has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can stimulate hair growth and help to prevent hair loss. Using lavender tincture as a hair rinse or adding a few drops to your favorite hair care products can help to keep your hair looking its best.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce scalp inflammation, which can be a cause of frizzy hair. This property is due to the presence of linalyl acetate and linalool.

Hair growth promotion: English lavender tincture can help promote hair growth, reducing the appearance of frizz by making hair look fuller and healthier. This is due to the presence of linalyl acetate and lavandulol.
Moisturizing: English lavender tincture has moisturizing properties that can help hydrate the hair, reducing frizz caused by dryness. This is due to the presence of polysaccharides.

Scalp soothing: English lavender tincture can help soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and dandruff, both of which can contribute to frizzy hair. This is due to the presence of linalool and lavandulol.

Antimicrobial: English lavender tincture's antimicrobial properties can help maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the overgrowth of bacteria and fungi, which can lead to frizzy hair. This is due to the presence of linalyl acetate and eucalyptol.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and nutrients, English lavender flowers help nourish the hair follicles, reducing frizz and promoting shine.
Leaves: Containing essential oils, lavender leaves help condition the hair, making it more manageable and less prone to frizz.
Buds: High in anti-inflammatory properties, lavender buds soothe the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 oz of fresh English lavender flowers and loosely pack them into a clean glass jar.
Pour 8 oz of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the lavender flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Securely fasten a lid on the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, gently shaking the jar every few days.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or muslin cloth, squeezing out the excess liquid to extract all the beneficial compounds.

Transfer the strained lavender tincture to a dark glass bottle, seal it, and store it in a cool, dark place; it will last for up to a year. To use as a hair rinse, dilute the tincture with water (1:1 ratio) and pour it over freshly washed hair, massaging it into the scalp and strands before rinsing with water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: High mallow tincture helps with frizzy hair because it contains mucilage, a type of plant compound that can moisturize and smooth the hair cuticle. This results in reduced frizz and increased shine. Additionally, high mallow is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to protect the hair from damage caused by environmental stressors and promote a healthy scalp. This herbal remedy can also help to detangle and improve the overall manageability of hair, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution to frizzy hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: High mallow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and flavonoids, can help reduce scalp inflammation, which can contribute to frizzy hair.
Emollient: The emollient properties of high mallow tincture, from mucilaginous compounds, help to smooth and soften hair strands, reducing frizz and increasing shine.
Moisturizing: High mallow tincture's moisturizing properties, due to its mucilage content, can hydrate the hair and scalp, preventing dryness that can lead to frizzy hair.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of high mallow tincture, from flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the hair from environmental damage and oxidative stress, which can cause frizz and hair breakage.

Soothing: High mallow tincture's soothing properties, due to its mucilaginous and demulcent nature, can alleviate itchiness and irritation on the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment that helps reduce frizz and support hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients, leaves help to nourish and smooth frizzy hair.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants that promote hair growth and reduce hair breakage.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, seeds help to moisturize and strengthen frizzy hair.
Roots: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a dry, itchy scalp and reduce frizz.
Bark: Contains tannins that can help to add shine and reduce frizz in hair.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of dried high mallow herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged, and tightly seal the jar.
Let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean jar or bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the high mallow tincture in a cool, dark place and use a few drops on frizzy hair daily or as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frizzy hair with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of rosemary, sage, and nettle tinctures. These herbs are known to promote hair growth, reduce dandruff, and add shine to your hair, thereby reducing frizz. Mix equal parts of these tinctures and dilute the solution with an equal amount of water. Massage the mixture onto your scalp and let it sit for 20-30 minutes before rinsing it out. Regular use of this treatment will result in smoother, more manageable hair. Always remember to perform a patch test before applying any new hair treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frizzy hair that are treated with herbal tinctures include dry, damaged, or brittle hair. Certain herbs, like rosemary, nettle, and horsetail, are often used in tinctures to promote hair health. Rosemary tincture can help stimulate hair growth and improve circulation in the scalp, while nettle tincture can strengthen hair and reduce hair loss. Horsetail tincture is rich in silica and can help improve hair's elasticity and shine, reducing the appearance of frizz. These tinctures are typically taken orally, but they can also be diluted and used as a hair rinse. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy.
